stem: Nutrition is an important medical science that has a significant place in which educational programme?
Which of the following is the FIRST era in the history of nutrition?
Lavoisier studied all of the following EXCEPT:
Which philosopher made the famous statement: 'Thou shouldst eat to live; not live to eat'?
The Cellular or Molecular Era began in which year?
Which of the following diseases listed in the text is a result of nutrient deficiency that affects vision?
During the Chemical-Analytical Era, which animals were used for the first quantitative studies on respiration?
Human nutrition deals with the provision of essential nutrients in food that are necessary to support which of the following?
The science of nutrition is recognized as a distinct discipline only in which year?
According to the text, which of the following correctly describes 'Nutrition'?
options: The chemical process of food breakdown in the intestines
The sum of processes by which a living organism receives materials from its environment and uses them to promote its own vital activities
The measurement of caloric content in food
The biological analysis of carbohydrates and fats

stem: Graham Lusk secured which benefit specifically for adolescent children?
options: A food allowance equal to that of adults

stem: According to the text, what is the LAST stage of the nutritional process in human nutrition?
The Biological Era of nutrition spans which period?
Which of the following nutrients was Lavoisier particularly interested in during the Chemical-Analytical Era?
In which country is Graham Lusk recognized as the founder of the science of nutrition?
The teaching and preparation techniques for food during the Naturalistic Era came from which source?
options: Trial and error and an incredible capacity of human inventiveness

stem: According to the text, which of the following best defines 'nutrients'?
options: Chemical substances produced in the liver
Materials received from the environment used by organisms to promote vital activities; it is food at work in the body
Hormones secreted by the endocrine glands

stem: Poor nutrition was specifically listed in the text as able to cause which thyroid-related deficiency disease?
After the 1960s, nutrition research shifted its focus to which of the following areas?
options: The discovery of new essential dietary components
The study of interrelationship among nutrients, precise roles, dietary requirements, and effect of processing on nutrient quality
The caloric value of carbohydrates and fats

stem: What is the responsibility of every nursing student according to the text?
options: To prescribe dietary supplements to all patients
To apply the principles of nutrition personally and pass the knowledge to others to keep a far-reaching nutritional improvement program alive
To conduct research on nutritional deficiency diseases
To manage the dietary department of a hospital

stem: Which nutritional stage comes immediately AFTER absorption in the nutritional process?
Hippocrates believed that food impacts a person's health, body and mind in order to achieve which goals?
options: Help prevent illness and maintain wellness

stem: Which of the following is NOT listed as a deficiency disease caused by poor nutrition in the text?
The science of nutrition emerged after the development of which other branches of science?
The Chemical-Analytical Era in nutrition history ends in which year?
According to the text, what was the major characteristic of the Naturalistic Era?
options: Scientific study of vitamins and minerals
Vague ideas about food mostly revolving around taboos, magical powers or medicinal value
Use of laboratory animals for nutritional experiments
Development of dietary charts for different age groups

stem: What was one key contribution of the Biological Era regarding dietary essentials?
options: It identified caloric values of carbohydrates
It demonstrated that there was another dietary essential beside carbohydrates, fats and proteins
It introduced molecular studies of nutrients
It emphasized the role of fiber in digestion

stem: Laplace, who collaborated with Lavoisier, held what professional title?
The history of nutrition is divided into how many eras?
According to the text, what does awareness and application of nutrition in medical practice have on healthcare in society?
options: An impact limited to pediatric care only

stem: A 28-year-old pregnant woman is brought to the antenatal clinic appearing pale and fatigued. On questioning, she admits to eating very little over the past three months due to poverty. Which nutrient deficiency-related condition listed in NSC 204 is she MOST likely at risk of developing?
A nursing student is asked by her supervisor why nutrition is important in nursing education. Based on NSC 204, which of the following is the MOST appropriate answer she should give?
options: It helps nurses to prescribe medications for dietary disorders
It equips nurses to promote health maintenance, prevent disease, facilitate recovery from illness, and augment treatment of disease states
It is required only for nurses working in the dietary department
It helps nurses understand the chemistry of food production

stem: A health educator is teaching a community group about the history of nutrition. She describes an era during which 'research changed from a search for essential dietary components to a study of the interrelationship among nutrients.' Which era is she referring to?

A 6-year-old child is brought to the paediatric ward showing signs of growth retardation. The mother reports the child has been feeding on a very unbalanced diet. Based on NSC 204 principles, which founding nutritionist's work specifically addressed securing adequate nutrition for children's normal growth?
A nurse educator is discussing with student nurses that nutrition knowledge serves a 'two-fold purpose.' A student asks for the two purposes. Which of the following is the CORRECT response?
options: To prepare food and to administer intravenous fluids
To provide knowledge useful in personal living and for professional training
To prescribe vitamins and to manage nutritional wards
To prevent disease and to perform nutritional surgery

stem: A public health nurse is conducting a nutritional awareness campaign in a rural community. She explains that 'food is at work in the body.' Which term in nutritional science does this phrase describe?

During a ward round, a nurse observes a patient with scurvy admitted due to poor dietary intake. Which era in the history of nutrition FIRST gave concentrated scientific interest to the caloric value of foods?

A professor presenting a lecture on the history of nutrition quotes an ancient thinker who said 'Let thy food be thy medicine and thy medicine be thy food.' Which historical figure made this statement?

A newborn is delivered prematurely to a mother who had been malnourished throughout the pregnancy. Based on the text, which condition directly associated with nutritional deficiency does this represent?

A nursing student states that 'nutrition is relatively a youngster in the scientific community.' Based on the text, this is because it was recognized as a distinct discipline only in 1934 and emerged only after the development of which other disciplines?
The fourth and current era of nutrition history is the _______________ era.
Graham Lusk is known as the founder of the science of nutrition in America and he lived from 1866 to _______________.
The Naturalistic Era of nutrition dates back to the _______________ of humanity.
In 1912, it was well established that there was another dietary essential beside carbohydrates, fats and _______________.
Lavoisier is known as the father of _______________ .
The nutritional stages in human nutrition are ingestion, digestion, absorption, transport, _______________ and excretion.
During the Naturalistic Era, early peoples had vague ideas about food that mostly revolved around taboos, magical powers or _______________ value.
Lavoisier, with the Physicist Laplace, used guinea pigs for the first _______________ studies on respiration.
The science of nutrition emerged only after the development of other branches of science like chemistry, _______________ , etc.
Millions of children were indebted to _______________ for the privilege of normal growth.
Hippocrates is known as the father of _______________ .
Awareness and application of nutrition in medical practice has a _______________ impact on health care in our society.
During the Chemical-Analytical Era, there was concentrated interest in the energy value or _______________ value of foods.
One of Graham Lusk's chief contributions was a chart of the _______________ requirements of the different grade and age groups.
Nutrition is an important _______________ science and has an important place in nursing educational programme.
Nutrition may be described as the sum of the processes by which a living organism receives materials from its environment and uses them to promote its own _______________ activities.
For nursing students, the study of nutrition fulfills a _______________ purpose.
The Chemical-Analytical Era was initiated by Lavoisier who studied respiration, oxidation and _______________ .
Nutrients are materials received from the environment; nutrition is food at work in the _______________ .
Poor nutrition can cause nutrient deficiency-related diseases such as blindness, anemia, scurvy, preterm birth, still birth, _______________ etc.
The Chemical-Analytical Era covers the period from 1750 to _______________.
Hippocrates realized that food impacts a person's health, body and mind to help prevent illness as well as maintain _______________.
After 1960, nutritional research changed from a search for essential dietary components to a study of the _______________ among nutrients.
Nurses need nutritional knowledge to augment the treatment of medical and _______________ disease states.
The Cellular or Molecular Era includes determination of human dietary requirements and the effect of _______________ on the nutrient quality of foods.
The science of nutrition is recognized as a distinct discipline only in _______________.
During the Naturalistic Era, nutrition was largely determined by the availability and _______________ of foods.
Every nursing student is responsible for applying the principle of nutrition personally and passing on knowledge to keep a far-reaching nutritional _______________ program alive.
Animals have continued to play a major role in _______________ experiments since the Chemical-Analytical Era.
The Naturalistic Era of nutrition spans from 400 BC to _______________ AD.
